title: penstemon caespitosus subsp. suffruticosus
intro: Penstemon caespitosus subsp. suffruticosus, commonly known as Rocky Mountain beardtongue or Little Beardtongue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is a perennial herb native to North America, valued for its medicinal properties due to the presence of iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, and is also considered to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies such as salves and infusions to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
